info.title: R Runner API
info.description: Authenticated API for running user-provided R scripts in an isolated execution environment and returning the resulting exit status, script console output, runtime diagnostics, and any generated files.

get.description: If RunRScript fails, use this to check whether the service is reachable and able to accept requests.

200.description: The service is available and responding normally.

post.description: Runs the supplied R script after pre-pulling the configured runtime image and returns the process outcome, exit code, captured script stdout/stderr, runtime stderr when the script fails to start, and any files produced during execution.

200.description: The request completed and the response contains the script result, including non-zero exit codes reported by the R process and runtime stderr only when the script could not start.

401.description: The request did not include a valid bearer token.

408.description: Execution exceeded the allowed runtime and was stopped.

413.description: The submitted script or one of the generated artifacts exceeded the configured size limit.

500.description: The service could not process the request because of a server-side configuration, image-pull, or runtime error.

ok.description: Indicates whether the service reports itself as healthy.

script.description: The complete R source code to execute.

filename.description: The filename assigned to the generated artifact.

mime_type.description: The MIME type describing the artifact content.

encoding.description: The encoding used for the artifact content field.

content.description: The artifact payload, represented either as plain UTF-8 text or as base64-encoded binary data, depending on the encoding field.

success.description: True when the R process exits with code 0; false when it exits with a non-zero code.

exit_code.description: The exit status returned by the R process.

stdout.description: All text written to the script's standard output during execution.

stderr.description: All text written to the script's standard error during execution.

runtime_stderr.description: Container runtime diagnostics. Empty when the script started successfully, even if the runtime emitted non-fatal warnings while preparing the container.

artifacts.description: Files produced by the script and captured by the runner for inclusion in the response.
